method.step_1: Represent each truck-day with stop-zone, GPS image, route distance, and fleet metadata.
method.step_2: Build stop-profile vectors and route-image clusters as evidence.
method.step_3: Ask the LLM to reason over statistical evidence and produce final pairwise truck correlations.
method.step_4: Normalize positive LLM-reasoned target-to-other correlations into truck appearance frequencies.
method.step_5: Sample target route clusters by target route frequency and other-truck routes by joint frequency.
method.truck_appearance_frequency: Positive target-to-other LLM-reasoned correlations normalized across other trucks.
method.route_appearance_frequency: Cluster days divided by selected period length.
method.joint_scenario_frequency: LLM truck appearance frequency multiplied by route appearance frequency.

1247.llm_rationale: Similar to 1312 pattern: strong stop correlation, purpose mismatch, moderate kms.

1311.llm_rationale: Negative stop correlation and purpose mismatch dominate despite high kms similarity.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch, kms high but not enough to offset

1312.llm_rationale: High stop-profile and kms similarity partially offset by purpose mismatch.

1350.llm_rationale: Good stop-profile and kms similarity with full purpose/duty match keep correlation solid.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose/duty match, kms closer than average

1363.llm_rationale: Moderate stop correlation but low kms similarity and purpose mismatch limit score.

1421.llm_rationale: Moderate stop-profile with strong metadata alignment (purpose, duty, kms) yields balanced similarity.

1423.llm_rationale: Same negative stop correlation pattern as 1311, low overall similarity.

1506.llm_rationale: Zero stop correlation, duty cycle mismatch, and purpose mismatch all indicate minimal similarity.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Highest among pairs but low weight impact
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Duty cycle mismatch and purpose mismatch drive low score

1575.llm_rationale: Very high kms similarity and solid stop correlation offset purpose mismatch.

1576.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile match, full purpose/duty alignment, moderate kms/age similarity support statistical score.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose and duty cycle fully match, kms moderately similar

1578.llm_rationale: High stop and kms similarity but purpose mismatch caps correlation.

1667.llm_rationale: Zero stop-profile correlation implies no meaningful behavioral link despite moderate kms similarity.

1687.llm_rationale: Weaker stop correlation and low kms/purpose alignment indicate limited similarity.

1688.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile correlation signals opposite temporal behavior despite matching purpose/duty; overall similarity remains low.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Slightly higher but insufficient to offset
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose/duty match but behavioral mismatch dominates

1785.llm_rationale: Strongly negative stop correlation outweighs matching purpose/duty, indicating divergent operation patterns.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose/duty match insufficient to raise similarity

1993.llm_rationale: Very high stop-profile correlation (0.86) suggests strong behavioral overlap despite purpose mismatch; adjusted slightly above raw statistical score.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch offsets some gain, duty cycle matches

2191.llm_rationale: High kms and stop similarity but low age similarity and purpose mismatch reduce total.

2201.llm_rationale: Strong stop correlation but weaker kms/age and purpose mismatch balance out.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Slightly higher than typical but minor weight

2518.llm_rationale: High stop correlation offset by low kms/age similarity and purpose mismatch.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Age and kms similarity low, purpose mismatch

final_scenario.sampling_method: For each scenario day, sample the target truck route from its own route_appearance_frequency, then sample one other truck route from joint_scenario_frequency = truck_appearance_frequency * route_appearance_frequency.
